clauses.clauseText: Section 11.2: Vendor liability for data breaches is limited to fees paid in the preceding 3 months.
clauses.riskExplanation: Extremely low liability cap fails to protect against costly breach notification, forensics, and regulatory fine exposure that can exceed $10M.
clauses.recommendedFix: Renegotiate to exclude cyber and data breach events from the general limitation of liability. Set a minimum floor of $5,000,000 for data breach indemnification.

clauses.clauseText: Section 8.4: Service Provider may utilize subprocessors without prior written consent, subject to maintaining equivalent security controls.
clauses.riskExplanation: Lack of prior consent mechanism for subprocessor engagement violates GDPR Article 28(2) and increases supply chain attack surface.
clauses.recommendedFix: Require 30-day prior written notification and right to object for any new subprocessor. Maintain an auditable subprocessor registry.

clauses.clauseText: Section 15.3: Either party may terminate without cause upon 60 days written notice.
clauses.riskExplanation: 60-day termination window is insufficient for critical infrastructure migration and may cause operational disruption.
clauses.recommendedFix: Extend termination without cause to 180 days with mandatory transition assistance period and data return obligations.

clauses.clauseText: Section 11.2: Vendor liability for data breaches is limited to fees paid in the preceding 3 months.
clauses.riskExplanation: Low liability cap fails to protect against costly breach notification and recovery expenses.
clauses.recommendedFix: Renegotiate to exclude cyber breaches from liability cap. Set minimum floor of $5,000,000.

clauses.clauseText: Section 8.4: Service Provider may utilize subprocessors without prior written consent.
clauses.riskExplanation: Violates GDPR Article 28(2) and increases supply chain attack surface.
clauses.recommendedFix: Require 30-day prior notification and right to object for any new subprocessor.

clauses.clauseText: Section 4.1: Target SLA availability is set to 99.5% computed monthly.
clauses.riskExplanation: 99.5% SLA permits over 3.6 hours of outage per month for payment processing.
clauses.recommendedFix: Renegotiate uptime target to 99.99% with service credits for SLA breaches.
